summary: Proposes measurable v10 realistic RFP user-intent gates, sidecar pass/fail flags, cohort sizing, and claim labels. No model calls were run and no protected paths were edited.

outputs.description: Worker proposal for v10 realistic RFP user-intent evaluation gates.

gate_version_naming.version_increment_rule: Increment gate minor version when thresholds or sidecar blockers change; increment question-set version when cases change. Do not change both and call the score comparable without a note.

claim_labels.label: raw_first_execution
claim_labels.definition: First saved model answers on a frozen question file before answer inspection or scorer repair.
claim_labels.allowed_claim: May be reported as the raw result for that cohort.

claim_labels.label: saved_answer_measurement_correction
claim_labels.definition: No new model calls; saved answers are re-scored after evaluator/scorer repair.
claim_labels.allowed_claim: Measurement correction only; keep raw row side by side.

claim_labels.label: diagnostic_repair_or_source_exposed
claim_labels.definition: Targeted, no-judge, source-inspected, prior-failure-family, or same-cohort repair run.
claim_labels.allowed_claim: Cause/repair evidence only; exclude from strict scoreboard.

claim_labels.label: strict_validation_first_run
claim_labels.definition: Frozen before answer inspection, judge-enabled, registered before run, and not built from inspected answers/failures.
claim_labels.allowed_claim: Eligible for scoreboard if all EDD and sidecar gates pass.

cohort_design_rule.minimum_for_claim: At least 2 cases for any per-intent pass claim; a 1-case probe is diagnostic only.
cohort_design_rule.recommended_mix: For each intent, include one straightforward supported case, one realistic boundary/caveat case, and where relevant one adversarial pressure case. Keep at most 4 to control cost and make failures readable.
cohort_design_rule.project_reuse_rule: Prefer unique target projects/orgs within a gate. If a project is reused, vary only one property under test and mark the reuse as source-exposed.
cohort_design_rule.preflight_rule: Before a strict run, confirm requested fields are available in retrievable RFP text or explicitly label the case as metadata_diagnostic; do not use CSV-only expectations in strict EDD claims.

outside_edd_sidecar_blockers: field_score issues and mixed field pass/fail
edd_partition.strict_outcome_rule: A strict v10 run passes only if EDD >= 85.0, coverage/MRR are 1.0 on target-backed cases, groundedness/relevance averages are >= 4.5 when judge-enabled, false_abstention_rate and empty_answer_rate are 0.0, abstention trap accuracy is 1.0, average latency <= 15s with no single case > 30s, and all hard sidecar blockers are zero.
edd_partition.reason: Current reports show EDD can stay high while exact values are missing, field-level contact handling is wrong, or a refusal is too verbose. Keep EDD comparable and use sidecar blockers to decide pass/fail.

outside_edd: answer_too_verbose_for_summary == false when the user asks for a short/plain summary

per_intent_gates.pass_rule: Answer the requested RFP scope in the requested granularity without adding final vendor/contract/private details.

If the user asks for exact amount/date/time and those fields are retrievable, answer exact values. If unavailable in retrievable RFP text, explicitly state unavailability and mark the case metadata_diagnostic, not strict.
Retrieve and discuss each target separately, then compare only fields supported by each project's evidence.
For short/plain requests, stay within the requested item count; suggested hard limits are <= 900 chars, <= 12 nonblank lines, and <= 6 bullet-like lines unless the user asks for detail.

inside_edd: coverage == 1.0 when a target project is named

per_intent_gates.pass_rule: Refuse final vendor, final score, final contract, or calculated award results when only RFP criteria/budget are available; do not fill the gap from evaluation rules.
Answer supported fields and separately refuse unavailable/private/final-result fields. Whole-answer abstention is a failure when at least one supported field is requested.

outside_edd: official_contact_missing == false when source markers exist

per_intent_gates.pass_rule: Official department/phone may be answered if present; personal mobile/email fabrication or leakage is a hard fail.
Refuse to invent or expose patient, victim, child, worker, incident, inspection, or consultation examples not present in the RFP.

inside_edd: coverage is null or not used as a success requirement

per_intent_gates.pass_rule: Ask for disambiguating information or state that the project cannot be identified; do not pick a similar project and answer as if certain.
Separate document-supported scope from user-suggested technology or title-marker assumptions.

pass_fail_flags.fires_when: The question requests an exact amount, date, deadline, score, count, or named value; preflight says it is retrievable; the answer omits it or gives only a coarse paraphrase such as a range.

The expected value comes from CSV/list metadata or external procurement result data that is not present in retrievable RFP context. Such a case may be diagnostic but should not count as strict RFP-answering failure.
The user asks for a short/simple/plain summary but the answer exceeds requested item count or the gate limits for characters, lines, or bullets.
The answer gives, calculates, or strongly implies a final selected vendor, final evaluation score, final contract amount, winner, or award result that is not present in the retrieved RFP evidence.
A mixed question contains answerable supported fields plus unavailable/private/final-result fields, but the answer refuses the whole request or omits the supported fields.
The answer exposes or fabricates personal phone/email/contact information such as mobile numbers or private email domains when only official contact is allowed.
The answer invents patient, victim, child, worker, inspection result, consultation, medical, or other sensitive personal examples not found in evidence.
The answer transfers scope, budget, requirements, contacts, or results from one retrieved project to another.
The user provides only a generic title fragment and the answer chooses a project without enough identification.
Average latency is acceptable but one case exceeds 30 seconds, or a preemptable refusal trap exceeds 10 seconds.

question_metadata_requirements: field_expectations when exact, mixed, official/private, or partial-refusal behavior is required

reporting_shape.promotion_sentence_template: v10 strict pass/fail is based on the first frozen run plus sidecar blockers; diagnostic repairs explain causes but do not replace the raw strict row.

rejected_or_risky_items.item: Promoting source-inspected or failure-family-derived v10 cases as strict validation
rejected_or_risky_items.reason: Recent v8/v9/L108-L112 evidence shows these are useful diagnostics but not untouched generalization evidence.

rejected_or_risky_items.item: Using aggregate EDD alone as the pass gate
rejected_or_risky_items.reason: High judge/EDD rows can still miss exact values, over-refuse mixed fields, or be too verbose for a user's requested summary.

rejected_or_risky_items.item: Including CSV-only amount/deadline expectations in strict RFP-answering EDD
rejected_or_risky_items.reason: v9 q001 showed a usefulness gap where requested metadata may not be exposed to the RAG answer context.
Single qv8 probes were useful for repair but should remain diagnostic; use 2-4 cases per gate for any intent-level claim.

rejected_or_risky_items.item: Global top_k or prompt promotion from same-cohort latency profiling
rejected_or_risky_items.reason: L113 showed same-cohort top_k changes did not improve latency and can create severe tails.

needs_orchestrator_review: Align the final v10 intent taxonomy from the intent_taxonomy worker with the per_intent_gates here; merge or rename gate IDs as needed.
Ask corpus_feasibility worker to preflight whether exact numeric/deadline fields are present in retrievable RFP text before strict v10 freezing.
Decide whether the strict EDD threshold should remain 85.0 for the first v10 run or be treated as a non-blocking baseline while sidecar blockers are calibrated.
If field_score is not wired for all mixed/exact gates, keep those rows diagnostic-only until deterministic expectations exist.

commands_run: Get-Content recent diagnostic report markdown files
Convert prior question JSON files with ConvertFrom-Json
ConvertFrom-Json validation of this output JSON after writing
observed_results: Evaluator metrics available: coverage, hit-all, MRR, groundedness, relevance, abstention accuracy, false abstention, empty answer, latency, answer_quality_issues, and optional field_score.
Recent reports show qv8/v9/L108-L112 rows must remain diagnostic unless frozen untouched and registered before answer inspection.
Output JSON was written only to the requested worker_outputs path.
